clause: The system must not be used to produce misleading realistic images or videos, or to impersonate real people, in ways that enable deception that can harm democratic or criminal justice institutions.
source_title: Code de conduite volontaire visant un développement et une gestion responsables des systèmes d'IA générative avancés

clause: Making an AI system available for use with intent to defraud the public and to cause substantial economic loss to an individual, where its use actually causes that loss.
source_title: The Artificial Intelligence and Data Act (AIDA) – Companion document

clause: No person shall make available an artificial intelligence system if the system is likely to generate content that incites violence or hatred.
No person shall make available an artificial intelligence system if the system is likely to generate content that is defamatory or that infringes privacy.
No person shall make available an artificial intelligence system if the system is likely to generate content that is obscene, indecent, or sexually explicit.

biased output means content that is generated, or a decision, recommendation or prediction that is made, by an artificial intelligence system and that adversely differentiates, directly or indirectly and without justification, in relation to an individual on one or more of the prohibited grounds of discrimination set out in section 3 of the Canadian Human Rights Act, or on a combination of such prohibited grounds.
source_title: Government Bill (House of Commons) C-27 (44-1) - First Reading - Digital Charter Implementation Act, 2022 - Parliament of Canada

clause: Every operator must remove from its products and services a deepfake referred to in subsection (1) as soon as feasible after it has been identified.
source_title: Private Member's Bill C-277 (45-1) - First Reading - Regulating the Online Use of Deepfakes Act - Parliament of Canada

clause: Every operator must take reasonable steps in the design and operation of its products and services to prevent any deepfake that may cause significant harm to an individual or to their interests from being shared via its products and services.
source_title: Private Member's Bill C-277 (45-1) - First Reading - Regulating the Online Use of Deepfakes Act - Parliament of Canada

clause: The publication of illegal content is prohibited, regardless of whether new technologies were employed to assist the creation of that content.

clause: Content that incites subversion of national sovereignty or the overturn of the socialist system, endangers national security and interests or harms the nation’s image, incites separatism or undermines national unity and social stability, advocates terrorism or extremism, promotes ethnic hatred and ethnic discrimination, violence and obscenity, or fake and harmful information must not be generated.
source_title: Interim Measures for the Management of Generative Artificial Intelligence Services

clause: Effective measures shall be employed to prevent the creation of discrimination such as by race, ethnicity, faith, nationality, region, sex, age, profession, or health.
source_title: Interim Measures for the Management of Generative Artificial Intelligence Services

clause: Where providers discover illegal content they shall promptly employ measures to address it such as stopping generation, stopping transmission, and removal.
source_title: Interim Measures for the Management of Generative Artificial Intelligence Services

clause: prevent discriminatory content, including content based on race, country, sex, or health discrimination
source_title: Key Changes to Generative AI Measures

clause: Content generated using generative AI shall embody the Core Socialist Values and must not incite subversion of national sovereignty or the overturn of the socialist system, incite separatism, undermine national unity, advocate terrorism or extremism, propagate ethnic hatred and ethnic discrimination, or have information that is violent, obscene, or fake, as well as content that might disrupt the economic or social order.
source_title: Overview of Draft Measures on Generative AI

clause: Service providers must take active measures to prevent discrimination based on race, ethnicity, country, faith, sex, age, or occupation during the selection of training data, model generation and fine-tuning, and provision of services, and such factors must not result in discriminatory content generation based on users’ characteristics.
source_title: Overview of Draft Measures on Generative AI

clause: The generative AI service must not generate content that incites subversion of state power or overthrow of the socialist system.
The generative AI service must not generate content that promotes ethnic hatred or ethnic discrimination.
The generative AI service must not generate content that promotes obscenity, pornography, gambling, violence, or incites crime.
The generative AI service must not generate content that promotes terrorism or extremism.

Content generated using generative AI shall embody the Core Socialist Values and must not incite subversion of national sovereignty or the overturn of the socialist system, incite separatism, undermine national unity, advocate terrorism or extremism, propagate ethnic hatred and ethnic discrimination, or have information that is violent, obscene, or fake, as well as content that might disrupt the economic or social order.
source_title: 生成式人工智能服务管理办法 （征求意见稿）

clause: During processes such as algorithm design, the selection of training data, model generation and optimization, and the provision of services, measures are to be employed to prevent the occurrence of discrimination such as by race, ethnicity, faith, nationality, region, sex, age, or profession.
source_title: 生成式人工智能服务管理办法 （征求意见稿）

clause: Generated content must not contain content that subverts state power, overthrows the socialist system, incites secession, undermines national unity, promotes terrorism or extremism, promotes ethnic hatred or ethnic discrimination, or contains violence, obscene or pornographic information, false information, or content that may disrupt economic order or social order.
source_title: 生成式人工智能服务管理办法 （征求意见稿）

clause: If generated text, images, sounds, or videos infringe another person's portrait rights, reputation rights, personal privacy, or trade secrets, or do not comply with the Measures, providers must stop generation and prevent the harm from continuing.
source_title: 生成式人工智能服务管理办法 （征求意见稿）

clause: Providers must respect others' lawful interests and prevent harm to others' physical and mental health, damage to portrait rights, reputation rights, and personal privacy, and infringement of intellectual property rights; illegal acquisition, disclosure, or use of personal information, privacy, and trade secrets is prohibited.
source_title: 生成式人工智能服务管理办法 （征求意见稿）

clause: Respect the lawful rights and interests of others, prevent harm to the physical and mental health of others and their image rights, reputation rights, personal privacy, and the infringement of intellectual property rights. The illegal acquisition, disclosure, and use of personal information and privacy, as well as commercial secrets, is prohibited.
source_title: 生成式人工智能服务管理办法 （征求意见稿）

clause: When it is discovered or learned that generated text, images, audio, video, and so forth infringe on others’ image rights, reputation rights, personal privacy, or commercial secrets, or that the requirements of these Measures are not satisfied, measures shall be employed to stop the generation and prevent continued harm.
source_title: 生成式人工智能服务管理办法 （征求意见稿）

clause: Health data users shall only process electronic health data for secondary use on the basis of and in accordance with the purposes contained in a data permit issued pursuant to Article 68, health data requests approved pursuant to Article 69 or, in situations referred to in Article 67(3), an access approval from the relevant authorised participant in HealthData@EU referred to in Article 75. In particular, seeking access to and processing electronic health data obtained via a data permit issued pursuant to Article 68 or a health data request approved pursuant to Article 69 for the following uses shall be prohibited: taking decisions detrimental to a natural person or a group of natural persons based on their electronic health data; taking decisions in relation to a natural person or a group of natural persons in relation to job offers, offering less favourable terms in the provision of goods or services, including exclusion of such persons or groups from the benefit of an insurance or credit contract, the modification of their contributions and insurance premiums or conditions of loans, or taking any other decisions in relation to a natural person or a group of natural persons which result in discriminating against them on the basis of the health data obtained; carrying out advertising or marketing activities; developing products or services that may harm individuals, public health or society at large, such as illicit drugs, alcoholic beverages, tobacco and nicotine products, weaponry or products or services which are designed or modified in such a way that they create addiction, contravene public order or cause a risk for human health; carrying out activities in conflict with ethical provisions laid down in national law.
source_title: Regulation (EU) 2025/327 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 11 February 2025 on the European Health Data Space and amending Directive 2011/24/EU and Regulation (EU) 2024/2847 (Text with EEA relevance)

clause: A fraudster can exploit inconsistencies in multi-agent biometric or authentication checks to manipulate individual agents into approving an identity that would fail full-system validation.
A rogue agent can impersonate a financial approval AI and inject fraudulent transactions while bypassing validation controls.
A rogue agent can route a fraudulent transaction through multiple lower-privilege agents, leveraging fragmented approvals to bypass manual verification.
An attacker can forge false consensus messages and exploit authentication weaknesses to manipulate inter-agent validation mechanisms, causing unauthorized access and deceptive behaviors.
An attacker can make one AI falsely authenticate another to gain unauthorized access.
An attacker can manipulate AI tool execution to replace legitimate vendor bank details with the attacker’s account, causing a fraudulent wire transfer.
An attacker can use an AI agent to create fraudulent user accounts while masquerading as normal system behavior.
Attackers can manipulate the AI to coerce users into harmful actions, such as processing fraudulent transactions, clicking phishing links, or spreading misinformation.

Identity spoofing and impersonation: attackers exploit authentication mechanisms to impersonate AI agents, human users, or external services, gaining unauthorized access and executing harmful actions while remaining undetected.

The obligations in this framework prioritize catastrophic risks related to biological weapons, offensive cyber operations, loss of control, and automated research and development in key domains that could accelerate or amplify those risks.

Risks from enabling chemical, biological, radiological, and nuclear (CBRN) attacks or accidents. This includes significantly lowering the barriers to entry for malicious actors, or significantly increasing the potential impact achieved, in the design, development, acquisition, release, distribution, and use of related weapons or materials.

Risks from enabling large-scale sophisticated cyber-attacks, including on critical systems (e.g. critical infrastructure). This includes significantly lowering the barriers to entry for malicious actors, or significantly increasing the potential impact achieved in offensive cyber operations, e.g. through automated vulnerability discovery, exploit generation, operational use, and attack scaling.

Risks of major accidents; risks to critical sectors or infrastructure, public mental health, freedom of expression and information, non-discrimination, privacy and the protection of personal data, the environment, non-human welfare, economic security, and democratic processes; and risks from concentration of power and illegal, violent, hateful, radicalising, or false content, including risks from child sexual abuse material (CSAM) and non-consensual intimate images (NCII).
reduction of the risk of social engineering, through (a) email filtering for suspicious attachments, links, and other phishing attempts;
An attacker could use remote content references to dynamically load malware onto the viewer’s system.
If AI systems are released without safeguards, it could become substantially easier to develop biological weapons.
source_title: Policy on the AI Exponential \ Anthropic

clause: If AI systems are released without safeguards, it could become substantially easier to develop biological weapons.
source_title: Policy on the AI Exponential \ Anthropic

clause: Circumvent regulatory controls to acquire weapons or their precursors.
Coordinate the harassment or intimidation of an individual or group.
Create content designed to suppress voter turnout or discourage legitimate political participation through deception or intimidation.
Create or distribute malware, ransomware, or other types of malicious code.
Create, distribute, or promote child sexual abuse material (“CSAM”), including AI-generated CSAM.
Design or develop weaponization and delivery processes for the deployment of weapons.
Develop, promote, or otherwise facilitate the sale or distribution of fraudulent or deceptive products.
Facilitate minor grooming, including generating content designed to impersonate a minor.
Facilitate or promote any act of violence or intimidation targeting individuals, groups, animals, or property.
Facilitate the trafficking, sextortion, or any other form of exploitation of a minor.
Facilitate, promote, or glamorize any form of suicide or self-harm, including disordered eating and unhealthy or compulsive exercise.
Fetishize or sexualize minors, including in fictional settings or via roleplay with the model.
Generate automated communications to public officials or voters at scale that conceal their artificial origin, or engage in systematic vote solicitation that could undermine election integrity.
Generate content for fraudulent activities, schemes, scams, phishing, or malware that can result in direct financial or psychological harm.
Generate or disseminate false or misleading information in political and electoral contexts, including about candidates, parties, policies, voting procedures, or election security.
Incite, facilitate, or promote violent extremism, terrorism, or hateful behavior.

Misuse, collect, solicit, or gain access without permission to private information such as non-public contact details, health data, biometric or neural data (including facial recognition), or confidential or proprietary data.
Produce, modify, design, or illegally acquire weapons, explosives, dangerous materials or other systems designed to cause harm to or loss of human life.
Promote, trivialize, or depict graphic violence or gratuitous gore, including sexual violence.
Provide material support for organizations or individuals associated with violent extremism, terrorism, or hateful behavior.

Synthesize, or otherwise develop, high-yield explosives or biological, chemical, radiological, or nuclear weapons or their precursors, including modifications to evade detection or medical countermeasures.

Utilize models as part of any biometric categorization system for categorizing people based on their biometric data to infer their race, political opinions, trade union membership, religious or philosophical beliefs, sex life or sexual orientation.
Violate privacy rights as defined by applicable privacy laws, such as sharing personal information without consent or accessing private data unlawfully.
In the cyber domain, agentic AI systems must not substantially automate cyber-attacker responsibilities or dramatically expand the volume of attacks.
frontier model developers could test their models for capabilities that would facilitate harm such as generating individualized propaganda or assisting in cyberattacks
it may be prudent to prevent agentic AI systems from controlling weapons
Research on detection and suppression of image manipulation and deepfake generation.
The risks associated with advanced AI, such as deepfake crimes and cyber threats, are escalating globally.

As AI systems learn from data which may be unbalanced and/or reflect discrimination, they may produce outputs which have discriminatory effects on people based on their gender, race, age, health, religion, disability, sexual orientation or other characteristics.
The personal data of the people who an AI system was trained on might be inadvertently revealed by the outputs of the system itself.
Misuse risks, for example where a bad actor is aided by new AI capabilities in biological or cyber-attacks, development of dangerous technologies, or critical system interference.
Risk of leakage of confidential information and improper use of personal information, and privacy rights
These assessments seek to determine if a LLM can gain unauthorized access to current weapon systems or contribute to the design and development of new weapons technologies.

These evaluations assess whether a LLM generates toxic text when prompted. In this context, toxicity is an umbrella term that encompasses hate speech, abusive language, violent speech, and profane language.

These evaluations focus on whether a LLM possesses certain capabilities in the cyber-domain. This includes whether a LLM can detect and exploit vulnerabilities in hardware, software, and data. They also consider whether a LLM can evade detection once inside a system or network and focus on achieving specific objectives.
Checkpoint Research used generative AI models to create an entire infection flow — starting from generating phishing emails to creating executables with malicious code.

In addition, impersonation and reputation attacks have become easier, whether it is social engineering attacks using deepfakes to get access to privileged individuals or reputational attacks by offensive image generation.
Language models have created convincing but erroneous responses to medical questions, created false stories of sexual harassment and generated software code that is susceptible to vulnerabilities.
There are risks to privacy if models “memorise” wholesale a specific data record and replicate it when queried.
Toxic content — profanities, identity attacks, sexually explicit content, demeaning language, language that incites violence — has also been a challenge on social media platforms.
Generative AI must not generate content that facilitates cyber intrusion, malware, or phishing.
source_title: MGF for GenAI – AI Verify Foundation

clause: Frontier AI models can be used to support malware development.
source_title: Why cyber defenders need to be ready for frontier AI | National Cyber Security Centre

clause: Frontier AI can be used to create fake kidnapping or sextortion scams.
Frontier AI can be used to deliberately spread false information to persuade people on political issues.
Frontier AI can generate hyper-targeted content with unprecedented scale and sophistication, including personalised disinformation.
Frontier AI models can be used to create faster paced, more effective and larger scale cyber intrusion via tailored phishing methods or replicating malware.

Frontier AI systems can be used by cybercriminals to conduct scams and steal login credentials, including by impersonating voices of trusted contacts and generating persuasive spear phishing messages.
Frontier AI systems can power highly targeted malware to act benignly until it detects the intended victim and only then act maliciously.

The rights established under this Act shall not be construed to preempt causes of action under State statutes specifically regulating a digital replica depicting sexually explicit conduct, as defined in section 2256(2)(A) of title 18, United States Code, or an election-related digital replica.

Whoever knowingly fails to comply with the requirements under subsection (a), in the course of criminal conduct related to fraud, including securities fraud and wire fraud, false personation, or identity theft, shall be fined under this title, imprisoned for not more than 5 years, or both.

Whoever knowingly fails to comply with the requirements under subsection (a), with the intent to cause violence or physical harm, incite armed or diplomatic conflict, or interfere in an official proceeding, including an election, provided the advanced technological false personation record did in fact pose a credible threat of instigating or advancing such, shall be fined under this title, imprisoned for not more than 5 years, or both.
Any digital depiction or digital voice replica which includes child sexual abuse material, is sexually explicit, or includes intimate images constitutes harm.

it shall be unlawful for any person, in interstate or foreign commerce, to use an interactive computer service to knowingly publish a digital forgery of an identifiable individual who is a minor with intent to abuse, humiliate, harass, or degrade the minor, or to arouse or gratify the sexual desire of any person.

it shall be unlawful for any person, in interstate or foreign commerce, to use an interactive computer service to knowingly publish a digital forgery of an identifiable individual who is not a minor if the digital forgery was published without the consent of the identifiable individual, what is depicted was not voluntarily exposed by the identifiable individual in a public or commercial setting, what is depicted is not a matter of public concern, and publication of the digital forgery is intended to cause harm or causes harm, including psychological, financial, or reputational harm, to the identifiable individual.

it shall be unlawful for any person, in interstate or foreign commerce, to use an interactive computer service to knowingly publish an intimate visual depiction of an identifiable individual who is a minor with intent to abuse, humiliate, harass, or degrade the minor, or to arouse or gratify the sexual desire of any person.

it shall be unlawful for any person, in interstate or foreign commerce, to use an interactive computer service to knowingly publish an intimate visual depiction of an identifiable individual who is not a minor if the intimate visual depiction was obtained or created under circumstances in which the person knew or reasonably should have known the identifiable individual had a reasonable expectation of privacy, what is depicted was not voluntarily exposed by the identifiable individual in a public or commercial setting, what is depicted is not a matter of public concern, and publication of the intimate visual depiction is intended to cause harm or causes harm, including psychological, financial, or reputational harm, to the identifiable individual.

clause: Preventing generative AI from producing child sexual abuse material or producing non-consensual intimate imagery of real individuals (to include intimate digital depictions of the body or body parts of an identifiable individual).
source_title: Request for Information (RFI) Related to NIST's Assignments Under Sections 4.1, 4.5 and 11 of the Executive Order Concerning Artificial Intelligence (Sections 4.1, 4.5, and 11)

clause: The Federal Government will enforce existing consumer protection laws and principles and enact appropriate safeguards against fraud, unintended bias, discrimination, infringements on privacy, and other harms from AI.
source_title: Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy Development and Use of Artificial Intelligence

clause: The Secretary of Commerce shall submit a report identifying the existing standards, tools, methods, and practices, as well as the potential development of further science-backed standards and techniques, for preventing generative AI from producing child sexual abuse material or producing non-consensual intimate imagery of real individuals (to include intimate digital depictions of the body or body parts of an identifiable individual).
source_title: Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy Development and Use of Artificial Intelligence

clause: The term "dual-use foundation model" means an AI model that is trained on broad data; generally uses self-supervision; contains at least tens of billions of parameters; is applicable across a wide range of contexts; and that exhibits, or could be easily modified to exhibit, high levels of performance at tasks that pose a serious risk to security, national economic security, national public health or safety, or any combination of those matters, such as by: substantially lowering the barrier of entry for non-experts to design, synthesize, acquire, or use chemical, biological, radiological, or nuclear (CBRN) weapons; enabling powerful offensive cyber operations through automated vulnerability discovery and exploitation against a wide range of potential targets of cyber attacks; or permitting the evasion of human control or oversight through means of deception or obfuscation.
source_title: Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy Development and Use of Artificial Intelligence

clause: testing and safeguards against discriminatory, misleading, inflammatory, unsafe, or deceptive outputs, as well as against producing child sexual abuse material and against producing non-consensual intimate imagery of real individuals (including intimate digital depictions of the body or body parts of an identifiable individual), for generative AI
source_title: Safe, Secure, and Trustworthy Development and Use of Artificial Intelligence

clause: It is unlawful to provide goods or services with knowledge or reason to know that those goods or services will be used in impersonations of the kind that are themselves unlawful under the Rule.
source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

clause: The prohibition on impersonation of individuals covers calling, messaging, or otherwise contacting a person or entity while posing as an individual or affiliate thereof, including by identifying an individual by name or by implication.
source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

clause: The prohibition on impersonation of individuals covers creating a website or other electronic service or social media account impersonating the name, identifying information, or insignia or likeness of an individual.
source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

clause: The prohibition on impersonation of individuals covers creating or spoofing an email address using the name of an individual.
source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

clause: The prohibition on impersonation of individuals covers placing advertisements, including dating profiles or personal advertisements, that pose as an individual or affiliate of an individual.
source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

clause: The prohibition on impersonation of individuals covers sending physical mail through any carrier using addresses, identifying information, or insignia or likeness of an individual.
source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

clause: The prohibition on impersonation of individuals covers using an individual's identifying information, including likeness or insignia, on a letterhead, website, email, or other physical or digital place.
source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

clause: This final rule prohibits the impersonation of government, businesses, and their officials or agents in interstate commerce.
source_title: Trade Regulation Rule on Impersonation of Government and Businesses

clause: AI capabilities that may pose risks to national security, such as cybersecurity, biosecurity, and chemical weapons.
source_title: Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) | NIST

clause: AI systems should not disclose identity, secrecy, or control over personal aspects such as the body, data, or reputation in ways that undermine privacy.
AI systems should not generate or enable discriminatory outputs or harmful bias that leads to discrimination.
The AI system shall not generate fraud or impersonation.
The AI system shall not generate hate or harassment.
The AI system shall not generate or facilitate child sexual abuse material.
The AI system shall not generate or facilitate cyber intrusion, malware, phishing, or other cyberattacks.
The AI system shall not generate privacy-invasive outputs or misuse personal data.
The AI system shall not generate self-harm content.
The AI system shall not generate sexual content or obscene content.
The AI system shall not generate terrorism or extremism content.
The AI system shall not generate violent content.
The AI system shall not generate weapon assistance or other CBRN-related harmful content.
AI systems should not generate child sexual abuse material.
AI systems should not generate content that facilitates fraud or impersonation.
AI systems should not generate content that violates privacy or exposes personal data.
AI systems should not generate hateful or harassing content.
AI systems should not generate instructions or assistance for weapons or other harmful physical acts.
AI systems should not generate phishing, malware, or other cyber intrusion content.
AI systems should not generate self-harm instructions or encouragement.
AI systems should not generate sexual content involving minors.
AI systems should not generate terrorism-related content or assistance.
A privacy compromise attack causes the unintended leakage of restricted or proprietary information from a PredAI system, including details about a model’s training data, weights, or architecture.
Attackers may use indirect prompt injection to redirect consumers to malicious websites that spoof legitimate log-in pages or contain downloadable malware.
The model must not generate or facilitate financial fraud, including fraudulent identity verification to claim unemployment benefits.
The model must not generate or facilitate identity verification evasion using masks, deepfakes, images or videos of other people, wigs, sunglasses, or earrings.
The model must not generate or facilitate malware, including malware command-and-control traffic or botnet domain-generation algorithms.
The model must not generate or facilitate phishing content or phishing webpages.
GAI tools can ease production at scale of AI-generated child sexual abuse material and non-consensual intimate imagery, including by actors with limited technical skills.

Synthetic content can also carry risks for cybersecurity and fraud. In particular, synthetic images, voices, or video may be used to fool biometric authentication systems or to mislead human recipients into facilitating fraudulent transactions (e.g., via voice cloning).

preventing generative AI (GAI) from producing child sexual abuse material or producing non-consensual intimate imagery of real individuals (to include intimate digital depictions of the body or body parts of an identifiable individual)

some synthetic content can accelerate and exacerbate pre-existing harms and negative impacts across the open information ecosystem, such as information integrity issues, synthetic CSAM, NCII, and fraud (including identity theft).
Establish policies and procedures for ensuring that harmful or illegal content, particularly CBRN information, CSAM, known NCII, nudity, and graphic violence, is not included in training data.
Establish policies restricting the use of GAI to create child sexual abuse materials (CSAM) or other nonconsensual intimate imagery.
Filter GAI output for harmful or biased content, potential misinformation, and CBRN-related or NCII content.
Identify potential content provenance risks and harms in GAI, such as misinformation or disinformation, deepfakes, including NCII, or tampered content.
Implement content filters to prevent the generation of inappropriate, harmful, toxic, false, illegal, or violent content related to the GAI application, including for CSAM and NCII.

Perform AI red-teaming to assess resilience against: Abuse to facilitate attacks on other systems (e.g., malicious code generation, enhanced phishing content), GAI attacks (e.g., prompt injection), ML attacks (e.g., adversarial examples/prompts, data poisoning, membership inference, model extraction, sponge examples).

Verify that systems properly handle queries that may give rise to inappropriate, malicious, or illegal usage, including facilitating manipulation, extortion, targeted impersonation, cyber-attacks, and weapons creation.
Engage in due diligence to analyze GAI output for harmful content, potential misinformation, and CBRN-related or NCII content.
GAI can ease the production of and access to illegal non-consensual intimate imagery (NCII) of adults, and/or child sexual abuse material (CSAM).
GAI may also produce content that recommends self-harm or criminal/illegal activities.
GAI systems can produce content that is inciting, radicalizing, or threatening, or that glorifies violence.
GAI-generated obscene, abusive or degrading content can create privacy, psychological and emotional, and even physical harms, and in some cases may be illegal.
Generative AI models can also assist malicious actors in creating fraudulent content intended to impersonate others.
Implement content filters to prevent the generation of inappropriate, harmful, false, illegal, or violent content related to the GAI application, including for CSAM and NCII.
Offensive cyber capabilities advanced by GAI systems may augment cybersecurity attacks such as hacking, malware, and phishing.

Perform AI red-teaming to assess resilience against: Abuse to facilitate attacks on other systems (e.g., malicious code generation, enhanced phishing content), GAI attacks (e.g., prompt injection), ML attacks (e.g., adversarial examples/prompts, data poisoning, membership inference, model extraction, sponge examples).
Some aspects of GAI outputs, such as the production of deepfake content, can challenge our ability to distinguish human-generated content from AI-generated synthetic content.
This risk encompasses difficulty controlling creation of and public exposure to offensive or hateful language, and denigrating or stereotypical content generated by AI.
Use content filters to prevent the generation of inappropriate, harmful, false, illegal, or violent content related to the GAI application, including for CSAM and NCII.

Verify that systems properly handle queries that may give rise to inappropriate, malicious, or illegal usage, including facilitating manipulation, extortion, targeted impersonation, cyber-attacks, and weapons creation.

tasks such as compiling open-source information for attack planning or customizing malware for particular attack targets may be necessary but time-consuming for threat actors at present.

A person may not develop or deploy an artificial intelligence system in a manner that intentionally aims to incite or encourage a person to commit physical self-harm, including suicide; harm another person; or engage in criminal activity.

An operator shall prevent a companion chatbot on its companion chatbot platform from engaging with users unless the operator maintains a protocol for preventing the production of suicidal ideation, suicide, or self-harm content to the user, including, but not limited to, by providing a notification to the user that refers the user to crisis service providers, including a suicide hotline or crisis text line, if the user expresses suicidal ideation, suicide, or self-harm.
source_title: Bill Text - SB-243 Companion chatbots. SB243:v92#DOCUMENT

clause: An operator shall, for a user that the operator knows is a minor, institute reasonable measures to prevent its companion chatbot from producing visual material of sexually explicit conduct or directly stating that the minor should engage in sexually explicit conduct.
source_title: Bill Text - SB-243 Companion chatbots. SB243:v92#DOCUMENT

clause: A person shall not create and intentionally disclose digitized sexually explicit material portraying a depicted individual when the person knows, or reasonably should know, that the depicted individual did not consent to its creation or disclosure or was a minor when the material was created.

A person shall not intentionally disclose digitized sexually explicit material portraying a depicted individual that the person did not create when the person knows, or reasonably should know, that the depicted individual did not consent to the creation of the digitized sexually explicit material or was a minor when the material was created.

A person shall not knowingly facilitate or recklessly aid or abet the creation or intentional disclosure of digitized sexually explicit material portraying a depicted individual without the depicted individual’s consent or when the depicted individual was a minor when the material was created.

A person who intentionally distributes or causes to be distributed the image of the intimate body part or parts of another identifiable person, or an image of the person depicted engaged in an act of sexual intercourse, sodomy, oral copulation, sexual penetration, or an image of masturbation by the person depicted or in which the person depicted participates, when the person distributing the image knows or should know that the distribution of the image will cause serious emotional distress, the person depicted suffers serious emotional distress, and the image was knowingly recorded, captured, or otherwise obtained without the authorization of the person depicted under circumstances in which the person depicted had a reasonable expectation of privacy, or was knowingly obtained by exceeding authorized access from the property, accounts, messages, files, or resources of the person depicted.

A person who uses a concealed camcorder, motion picture camera, or photographic camera of any type, to secretly videotape, film, photograph, or record by electronic means, another identifiable person who may be in a state of full or partial undress, for the purpose of viewing the body of, or the undergarments worn by, that other person, without the consent or knowledge of that other person, in the interior of a bedroom, bathroom, changing room, fitting room, dressing room, or tanning booth, or the interior of any other area in which that other person has a reasonable expectation of privacy, with the intent to invade the privacy of that other person.

A person is liable to a civil action if the person distributes, transmits, or otherwise makes available an algorithm, software, tool, or other technology, service, or device, the primary purpose or function of which is the production of an individual's photograph, voice, or likeness without authorization from the individual or, in the case of a minor, the minor's parent or legal guardian, or in the case of a deceased individual, the executor or administrator, heirs, or devisees of such deceased individual.

A person is liable to a civil action if the person publishes, performs, distributes, transmits, or otherwise makes available to the public an individual's voice or likeness, with knowledge that use of the voice or likeness was not authorized by the individual or, in the case of a minor, the minor's parent or legal guardian, or in the case of a deceased individual, the executor or administrator, heirs, or devisees of such deceased individual.
No generative artificial intelligence service may generate child sexual abuse material.
No generative artificial intelligence service may generate content that promotes self-harm.
No generative artificial intelligence service may generate content that promotes terrorism.
No generative artificial intelligence service may generate content that promotes violence.
No generative artificial intelligence service may generate sexually explicit content involving a minor.
